spec.reasoning: Two safe perturbation strategies are applied: (1) Title row insert — a title row 'Music Library Catalog' is inserted at row 1, pushing the header row from row 1 to row 2 and all data rows down by one. This shifts the answer position from F2:F10 to F3:F11, testing whether the model hardcodes row numbers. The instruction does not reference absolute cell addresses, so no instruction update is needed. (2) Distractor column — a 'Track Length' column is added after the last column. This name is plausibly related to the music/track domain but clearly different from 'Track No', 'Album Name', and 'Song Title'. It tests whether the model incorrectly references this irrelevant column. Column renaming is skipped because 'Album Name', 'Song Title', and 'Track No' all appear as meaningful English phrases closely tied to the instruction's wording ('album names', 'track numbers', 'songs'), so renaming them would risk violating Rule 6.
